LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH AND BAKERSFIELD MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS DENIED. A WIDER VENUE CHOICE IS PERMITTED IN ACTIONS AGAINST CORPORATIONS OR OTHER ENTITIES THAN IN ACTIONS AGAINST INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. ACTIONS AGAINST A CORPORATION ARE TRIABLE: (1) WHERE IT HAS ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS (ITS "RESIDENCE" FOR VENUE PURPOSES); OR (2) WHERE THE CONTRACT WAS MADE OR TO BE PERFORMED (WHETHER SPECIFIED IN WRITING OR NOT); OR (3) WHERE THE OBLIGATION OR LIABILITY AROSE OR THE BREACH OCCURRED. [SEE CCP 395.5; ANAHEIM EXTRUSION CO. V. SUP.CT. (CLASSIC MOLDING CO.) (1985) 170 CA3D 1201, 120.] HERE, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IS IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY, THEREFORE SAN FRANCISCO IS THE PROPER VENUE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR NO. 5963; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

CARTER V. PRIME HEALTHCARE PARADISE VALLEY LLC (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 395 APPLIES TO CLAIMS BROUGHT UNDER WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE SECTIONS 15600 ET SEQ., WHETHER THE ALLEGED VICTIM IS AN ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT. PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD MORE THAN SIMPLE, GROSS, OR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE. PER CARTER, PLAINTIFFS' CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS OF RECKLESSNESS, OPPRESSION, FRAUD OR MALICE ARE INSUFFICIENT. PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS WITH PARTICULARITY THAT ESTABLISH THE ELEMENTS OF DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE AS SET OUT AT PAGES 406-7 OF CARTER. THE FACTS MUST BE SPECIFIC TO ABUMERI'S CONDUCT. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: IRENE L. TOFFT, CSR #12913. (302/HK)

RULING - The matter is uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL TINNISH, NANCY TINNISH's MOTION TO AMEND Complaint For Fictitious Name Regarding George Wahba, M.D. As Doe 1.

Plaintiffs Michael and Nancy Tinnish's motion to amend complaint for fictitious name for George Wahba is granted. Because the court is unable to determine as a matter of law whether plaintiffs were truly ignorant of their cause of action for battery against Dr. Wahba when they filed their complaint, plaintiffs are permitted to file their proposed amendment and Dr. Wahba may allege any statute of limitations or CCP 474 defenses he believes he has. Those defenses will then be adjudicated in the normal course. Proceeding in this manner is in accord with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings and will allow for adjudication of the CCP 474 issue raised by Dr. Wahba on a proper and full record. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcripts)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Plaintiff to file an amendmment to the Complaint.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Dignity Health Dba Mercy Hospitals Of Bakersfields Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tyler Saldo, Esq. (805-547-0700) of Saldo Law Group for Plaintiff Michael Tinnish and Nancy Tinnish through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA MERCY HOSPITALS OF BAKERSFIELD's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication

Defendant Dignity Health's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative for summary adjudication is denied as to the medical negligence and loss of consortium causes of action and granted as to the medical battery causes of action. Assuming without deciding that the expert opinions of Ms. Jun are admissible, the declaration of Dr. Segal creates triable issues whether Dignity's treatment fell below the standard of care and whether Dignity's negligence was a substantial factor in causing harm to plaintiff Michael Tinnish, thereby precluding judgment in favor of Dignity on the medical negligence and loss of consortium causes of action. Dignity's objections to Dr. Segal's objections are overruled. Dr. Segal affirmatively states in paragraphs 3 and 4 of his declaration that he is knowledgeable about the standard of care for operating room personnel, including surgical nurses, during lumbar spine surgery. All of the challenges to Dr. Segal's declaration go to the weight, not admissibility, of his opinions. The undisputed facts establish that Dr. Abumeri (possibly aided by Dr. Wahba), not Dignity's personnel, performed the surgical procedure that was allegedly not consented to by Mr. Tinnish, and thus Dignity is not liable for medical battery. (CACI 530A). (as fully reported in the reporter's transcripts)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAKERSFIELD NEUROSCIENCE & SPINE INSTITUTE'S DEMURRER TO the second cause of action for dependent adult abuse in the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Winn v. Pioneer Medical Group, Inc. (2016) 63 Cal.4th 148 holds that only a defendant with a substantial caretaking or custodial relationship for a plaintiff can be liable for dependent adult abuse. DEFENDANT BAKERSFIELD NEUROSCIENCE & SPINE INSTITUTE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant Bakersfield Neuroscience & Spine Institute's demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 395 applies to claims brought under Welfare and Institutions Code sections 15600 et seq., whether the alleged victim is an elder or dependent adult. Per Carter and the applicable code sections, to bring a cause of action for dependent adult abuse, the complaint must allege facts with particularity. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts with particularity that establish the elements of dependent adult abuse as set out at pages 406-7 of Carter. The facts must be specific to the Spine Institute's conduct. Order signed.

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jul-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tyler Saldo, of Law Offices of Steven L. Saldo, Tel. 805.547.0700, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Stephanie L. Roundy, of Riggio Mordaunt & Kelly, Tel. 209.473.8732, for defendants Bakersfield Neuroscience and Spine Institute, appearing via CourtCall;
Brenda M. Ligorsky, of Carroll, Kelly, Trotter, Franzen, McKenna & Peabody, Tel. 562.432.5855 ext 260, for defendants Dignity Health, appearing via CourtCall;
Ricardo Martinez, of McNamara, Ney, Beatty, Slattery, Borges & Ambacher, LLP, Tel. 925.939.5330, for defendant Timothy Wiebe, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH and BAKERSFIELD MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is denied. A wider venue choice is permitted in actions against corporations or other entities than in actions against individual defendants. Actions against a corporation are triable: (1) where it has its principal place of business (its "residence" for venue purposes); or (2) where the contract was made or to be performed (whether specified in writing or not); or (3) where the obligation or liability arose or the breach occurred. [See CCP 395.5; Anaheim Extrusion Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Classic Molding Co.) (1985) 170 CA3d 1201, 120.] Here, Defendant Dignity Health's principal place of business is in San Francisco County, therefore San Francisco is the proper venue. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
